title: caltha alpina
intro: Caltha alpina, also known as the alpine marsh marigold, is a flowering plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Caltha alpina include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil to be used in herbal remedies, with the roots and leaves being the primary components used in these preparations.
